steps.title: Subtract the equations
steps.content:
The second equation has one extra $y$ compared with the first equation:
$$x+y=105$$
$$x+y+y=115$$

steps.title: Find the extra amount
steps.content:
Subtract the first equation from the second:
$$(x+y+y)-(x+y)=115-105$$
$$y=10$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the two equations and read the intersection's $y$-coordinate. The answer is 10.
